tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'eyeglasses Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: You're in Kyoto right now — find shops today before moving to Takayama.
tasks.reward: Gets the shopping done early in trip so you can enjoy the rest without this hanging over you
nuggets: Jins and Zoff are major chains in Japan
Kyoto Station area has several optical shops
tasks.system_prompt: I'm in Kyoto right now (April 3, day 12 of my Japan trip) and need to find eyeglasses stores where I can buy 4 pairs before leaving Japan. I need stores that: (1) serve English speakers or have easy navigation for foreigners, (2) can do same-day or next-day service, (3) are in central Kyoto near tourist areas or Kyoto Station. Give me a list of 5-7 specific stores with addresses, Google Maps links, opening hours, and notes on whether they're budget-friendly chains (like Jins/Zoff) or higher-end boutiques. Also tell me if I need to bring anything specific (prescription paper format, measurements, etc.).

tasks.first_domino: Check insurance app or email agent subject 'Japan eyeglasses coverage'
tasks.hook_line: You need to know reimbursement rules before spending $400+ on 4 pairs.
tasks.reward: Ensures you get maximum insurance reimbursement and don't waste money
nuggets: US insurance often reimburses foreign optical purchases
Need itemized receipt in English if possible
Prescription format may need conversion for records
tasks.system_prompt: I'm buying 4 pairs of eyeglasses in Japan (budget ~$400 total) and need to understand: (1) What does my US vision insurance cover for international purchases? (2) What documentation do I need to collect (receipts, prescription format, etc.) to get reimbursed? (3) Are there common issues with claiming foreign optical purchases I should avoid? I have standard US vision insurance through my employer. Give me a checklist of things to ask my insurance company and things to request from the Japanese optical shop to maximize reimbursement.

tasks.first_domino: List must-see Kyoto spots on phone notes app
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are worth waking up early for?
tasks.reward: Turns remaining Kyoto days from aimless wandering into intentional exploration
nuggets: Philosopher's Path cherry blossoms peak now
tasks.system_prompt: I'm in Kyoto right now with Dana (April 3-5, about 2.5 days left here). We like temples, nature walks, good food, and avoiding crowds. Already saw Kinkaku-ji. Help me build a day-by-day itinerary with: (1) 2-3 major sights per day with opening hours and entry fees, (2) walking routes between them with Google Maps links, (3) recommended lunch/dinner spots nearby, (4) a mix of famous landmarks and hidden gems. We're staying in central Kyoto. Optimize for early morning starts to beat crowds and include at least one onsen or bathhouse experience.

tasks.hook_line: Mountain town vibes — old streets, sake breweries, and Japanese Alps views.
tasks.reward: Makes the most of the scenic mountain detour before Tokyo
nuggets: Takayama morning market famous for local food
Old town district walkable in 2-3 hours
Day trips to Shirakawa-go village possible
tasks.system_prompt: I'm visiting Takayama (Japanese Alps mountain town) for 1.5 days around April 6-7 with Dana. We're coming from Kyoto and heading to Tokyo after. What should we prioritize? Looking for: (1) Top 3-5 must-see/do things in Takayama itself, (2) Whether a day trip to Shirakawa-go is worth it (time, cost, logistics), (3) Local food specialties to try, (4) Any scenic walks or light hikes nearby. We like nature, traditional architecture, and local experiences. Give me a realistic itinerary with timing and transportation notes.

tasks.first_domino: List Tokyo neighborhoods and what each is known for
tasks.hook_line: Final 3 days in Tokyo — where do you want the trip to end?
tasks.reward: Creates a strong finish to the trip with intentional Tokyo experiences
nuggets: Tsukiji outer market for food tours
tasks.system_prompt: I'm spending the final 3 days of my Japan trip in Tokyo (April 7-9) with Dana. We'll have seen temples and nature by then, so we want Tokyo to feel different — modern, energetic, urban. Help me plan: (1) Which neighborhoods to prioritize (we like food, interesting shops, people-watching), (2) A mix of iconic Tokyo experiences (Shibuya crossing, Senso-ji, etc.) and local spots, (3) Good dinner/nightlife options, (4) Any unique Tokyo-only activities worth the time. We're staying in Shinjuku. Give me a loose 3-day structure with Google Maps links and timing estimates.

tasks.first_domino: Open terminal and clone the Cerebro repo
tasks.hook_line: The developer portal that's been parked for weeks — can you crack the local setup?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all Cerebro work and gets you back into active development
nuggets: Check README for Docker setup requirements
May need local database or test data
Ask team for .env template if not in repo
tasks.system_prompt: I'm trying to get Cerebro (our internal developer portal) running on my local machine. I haven't touched it in weeks. Help me: (1) Identify common setup blockers for developer portals (missing env vars, database seeds, API keys, service dependencies), (2) Create a checklist of what I need before starting (credentials, tools, configs), (3) Write a step-by-step setup script assuming I'm starting fresh. The project has been parked, so I need to rebuild context quickly. Optimize for getting to a 'Hello World' running state as fast as possible, even if features are broken.

recommended_slots.reason: Early morning in Kyoto — research eyeglasses shops now to visit when they open at 10am
Critical dependency for glasses shopping — validate insurance before spending money today
You're in Kyoto right now with 2.5 days left — planning today makes tomorrow better
